title: hibbertia glebosa subsp. oblonga
intro: Hibbertia glebosa subsp. oblonga, also known as the oblong Guinea flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dilleniaceae. It is native to Western Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi. Hibbertia glebosa subsp. oblonga can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas and infusions, and has also been used in traditional medicine for its potential to aid in the treatment of skin conditions and wounds.
